name: Fasold
description: Fasold, also known as Fafnir's father, is a complex figure in Germanic mythology, appearing prominently in the Volsunga Saga and related narratives. He is a giant, often described as greedy and powerful, whose actions have significant repercussions for the hero Sigurd and the Volsung clan. Fasold, along with his brothers Regin and Otr, demands wergild (man-price) from the gods Odin, Hoenir, and Loki after Loki kills Otr, who was in the form of an otter. The wergild is paid in gold, including the cursed treasure of Andvari. Fasold’s greed leads him to kill his own father, Hreidmarr, to possess all the gold, including the ring Andvaranaut, which carries a curse of destruction and misfortune. He then transforms himself into a dragon to guard his hoard, becoming the fearsome dragon Fafnir. Fasold’s transformation and guarding of the hoard ultimately set the stage for Sigurd's famous dragon-slaying. While technically not a hero in the conventional sense, Fasold's story is crucial to the heroic narrative of Sigurd, as his actions create the challenge that Sigurd must overcome. His avarice and treachery serve as a cautionary tale about the corrupting influence of wealth and the dangers of unchecked ambition. Fasold's transformation into a dragon and his eventual death at Sigurd's hand are pivotal moments in Germanic mythology, shaping the destinies of heroes and gods alike. He represents the destructive force of greed and the inevitable consequences of violating sacred laws and familial bonds. Fasold's legacy lies in the unfolding drama of the Volsung saga and the consequences of his fatal decisions.
subtitle: Greedy Giant, Dragon Hoarder, Volsung Foe
achievements: Served as an antagonist for Sigurd

keyMyths.title: The Otter's Ransom
keyMyths.description: Loki kills Otr, who is in otter form. Fasold and his brothers demand a ransom of gold to cover the otter's skin, which the gods must fill and cover with gold.

keyMyths.title: The Slaying of Hreidmarr
keyMyths.description: Fasold, driven by greed for the cursed gold, murders his father, Hreidmarr, to claim the entire treasure for himself, initiating the cycle of violence and tragedy.

keyMyths.title: Transformation into Fafnir
keyMyths.description: To protect his ill-gotten gains, Fasold transforms himself into a fearsome dragon, becoming the dragon Fafnir, guarding the treasure in the Gnita-Heath.

sources.author: Anonymous
